name: Verizon Wireless
industry_description: Formed 2000 from Bell Atlantic/GTE/AirTouch. Won massive C Block re-auction spectrum.

name: NextWave Telecom Inc.
industry_description: RESOLVED: Founded by Allen Salmasi. Bid $5.1B on PCS C-block (Auction 5, 1996) using DE credits. Defaulted, filed bankruptcy 1998. FCC tried to reclaim licenses — SUPREME COURT ruled for NextWave (FCC v. NextWave Personal Communications, 2003). Eventually sold spectrum to T-Mobile and Verizon. Most notorious spectrum speculator in FCC history. Contact John A. Dooley later appeared at 2014 AWS Spectrum Bidco Corp — the pattern repeats.

name: AT&T Wireless Services Inc.
industry_description: AT&T's wireless unit. Major Auction 35 bidder for C Block PCS.

name: Alaska Native Wireless LLC
industry_description: Entity that participated in FCC Auction 35 (C/F Block PCS, 2000). Financed by AT&T. Won 29 closed licenses for $2.6B, representing 36% of population-weighted spectrum in the closed auction. Competitors called Alaska Native Wireless and related entity Salmon PCS "shills" for AT&T and Cingular Wireless. Used Alaska Native Corporation designated entity status to access set-aside spectrum blocks reserved for small businesses. Became poster child for DE program abuse.

name: Cingular Wireless
industry_description: SBC-BellSouth wireless JV. Major buyer of defaulted C Block spectrum in re-auction.

name: SpectrumCo LLC
industry_description: RESOLVED: Cable consortium formed for Auction 66 (AWS-1). Original members: Comcast, Time Warner Cable, Cox Communications, Advance/Newhouse, Sprint (5%). Sprint sold stake 2007. Cox redeemed 2009. Final: Comcast 63.6%, TWC 31.2%, Bright House 5.3%. Won 137 licenses for $2.37B at $0.45/MHz-POP (lowest of all major bidders). Sold 122 licenses to VERIZON for $3.6B (Dec 2011) — $1.2B profit in 5 years. Deal included controversial cross-sell agreements creating cable/wireless detente that shaped industry for a decade. Comcast got ~$2.3B, TWC ~$1.1B, Bright House ~$189M.

name: WirelessCo LP
industry_description: Sprint PCS joint venture (1994) — Sprint, TCI, Comcast, Cox. Won PCS A/B block licenses.

name: Channel 51 License Co LLC
industry_description: RESOLVED: Wholly owned by Channel 51 Parent LLC (Delaware). Managed by Paul Chisolm. Investors: Australia's Future Fund (sovereign wealth, 48.21% equity via Future Fund Investment Co No.5 Pty Ltd), Columbia Capital (19.79% via Columbia Capital VI LLC + Columbia Spectrum LLC, Fleming/Siegel). Won 8 licenses for $859M in Incentive Auction. 600 MHz spectrum leased to T-Mobile, now being sold outright to T-Mobile. Section 310(b) foreign ownership petition filed due to Australian sovereign wealth fund stake.

name: AT&T Wireless Services
industry_description: AT&T's wireless subsidiary, major bidder in PCS auctions.

name: PCS PrimeCo LP
industry_description: Joint venture: AirTouch + Bell Atlantic + NYNEX + US West. All four eventually merged into Verizon Communications.

name: Frontier Wireless LLC
industry_description: EchoStar bidding entity for 700 MHz auction. Won majority of E block.

name: Cook Inlet Region Inc. (CIRI)
industry_description: Alaska native corporation. Major D/E/F block winner through designated entity program.

name: Pacific Bell Mobile Services
industry_description: Pacific Telesis wireless subsidiary, Auction 4 bidder.

name: MCI Communications Corporation
industry_description: Long distance giant. Won DBS auction (Auction 8) for $682.5M sole bidder.

name: King Street Wireless, L.P.
industry_description: RESOLVED: NTELOS subsidiary. NTELOS Holdings Corp was a Virginia regional telecom. Acquired by Shenandoah Telecommunications (Shentel) in 2016. King Street Wireless held 700 MHz licenses in Virginia/West Virginia/Kentucky.

name: T-Mobile USA Inc.
industry_description: Deutsche Telekom's US wireless subsidiary. Major PCS auction bidder.

name: GTE Mobilnet
industry_description: GTE's wireless subsidiary, Auction 4 bidder.

name: Spectrum Wireless Holdings, LLC
industry_description: CONFIRMED: Charter Communications subsidiary (appears in Charter SEC 10-K Exhibit 21.1). Delaware LLC. Won 210 CBRS PAL licenses in Auction 105 for $464M across 106 counties. Charter uses "Spectrum" branding for consumer wireless MVNO.

name: XF Wireless Investment, LLC
industry_description: CONFIRMED: Comcast Corporation subsidiary. Delaware LLC. "XF" = Xfinity branding. Won 830 CBRS PAL licenses in Auction 105 for $459M (top 5 winner). Spectrum supports Xfinity Mobile network densification alongside Verizon MVNO agreement.

name: Advantage Spectrum, L.P.
industry_description: RESOLVED: Delaware LP. USCC Wireless Investment Inc. (US Cellular subsidiary) is limited partner. Won 124 licenses in Auction 97 (AWS-3) for $338.3M net. Partnership structure used by US Cellular rather than bidding directly. US Cellular controlled by TDS (Carlson family). US Cellular being broken up 2025 — T-Mobile acquiring operations, AT&T/Verizon acquiring spectrum.

name: PageNet (Paging Network Inc.)
industry_description: Largest US paging company in the 1990s. Won nationwide narrowband PCS licenses in Auction 1.

name: Nextel Communications
industry_description: iDEN/SMR operator. Acquired 800 MHz spectrum through auctions and private purchases.

name: MetroPCS Communications Inc.
industry_description: Built prepaid wireless empire on auction-acquired spectrum. Major bidder in multiple auctions.

name: Pocket Communications Inc.
industry_description: C Block winner that eventually built CDMA network in San Antonio. Founded by Paul Posner.

name: GWI PCS Inc.
industry_description: RESOLVED: Founded 1994 by Roger D. Linquist and Malcolm Lorang (Richardson TX). Bid $1.06B on 14 C-Block PCS licenses (Auction 10, 1996). Filed Chapter 11 Jan 1998 — court reduced $954M FCC obligation to $166M. Emerged from bankruptcy, REBRANDED AS METROPCS. Launched first network in Dallas 2002. T-Mobile merged with MetroPCS 2013. Most successful "phoenix" in FCC auction history.

name: 2014 AWS Spectrum Bidco Corporation
industry_description: PARTIALLY RESOLVED: Delaware corp (#5601693). Address: One International Place Suite 1400, Boston MA 02110 (PE district). Contact: John A. Dooley — ALSO connected to NextWave Wireless (most notorious spectrum speculator in FCC history). Filed Section 310(b)(4) petition = significant FOREIGN OWNERSHIP requiring FCC approval. Won licenses in Auction 97 with $291.8M net. "Bidco" = classic PE leveraged buyout naming. Foreign capital source UNIDENTIFIED. Warrants further investigation.

name: BellSouth Personal Communications
industry_description: BellSouth wireless subsidiary, major Auction 4 bidder.

name: Denali Spectrum, LLC
industry_description: RESOLVED: Doyon Ltd (Alaska Native Corp, 17.5% control) + Leap/Cricket (82.5% equity). Won Great Lakes AWS-1 license (Chicago/Minneapolis/Milwaukee, 58M POPs) for $274M net after DE credits. Leap acquired Doyon's stake 2010. AT&T acquired Leap 2014. Same Doyon structure later used by DISH for Northstar (Auction 97).

name: Various IVDS Licensees
industry_description: Multiple small entities won IVDS licenses. 178 bidders won 594 licenses.

name: Dobson Communications
industry_description: Rural wireless carrier (Oklahoma). Acquired by AT&T 2007 for $2.8B.

industry_description: Ameritech's wireless division, Auction 4 bidder.

name: Canopy Spectrum, LLC

INVESTORS: GEM Realty Capital (Chicago), M/C Partners (Boston PE, communications focus), Shamrock Capital Advisors (LA — originally the DISNEY FAMILY investment arm, Roy E. Disney's trust). Won 84 C-Band licenses for ~$172M in Auction 107 across 43 markets (5th largest by license count). Legitimate PE spectrum vehicle, not DE abuse.

name: Aloha Partners LP
industry_description: RESOLVED: Founded 2000, Providence RI. CEO: Charlie Townsend. Co-founder: Thomas Sullivan (TeleCorp PCS). Largest buyer of 700 MHz in Auctions 31 (2001) and 44 (2003). Accumulated 12 MHz covering 60% of US, all top 10 markets. Also acquired LIN Television's 31 700 MHz licenses (2007). SOLD TO AT&T MOBILITY FOR $2.5 BILLION (FCC approved Feb 4, 2008). Enormous return on investment. Townsend went on to create Whitewater Wireless for next auction cycle. Pattern: acquire at auction, sell to AT&T.

Won 30 AWS licenses + 35 700 MHz licenses (~47M POPs east of Mississippi). Described as part of "shadowy world of spectrum speculators" — acquired spectrum with no plan to build. AT&T agreed to acquire Aug 2012 but deal CANCELLED. Licenses sold off piecemeal. Ridgeland MS = same city as C Spire HQ.

Basis of NextNav petition to reconfigure entire Lower 900 MHz band.

industry_description: RESOLVED: Vincent McBride — former US Postal Service mail carrier turned spectrum dealer. One-man operation averaging $1.2M annually buying/selling FCC licenses. Also operates as McBride Spectrum Partners LLC. McLean VA area. Qualified bidder in Auctions 101 (28 GHz), 102 (24 GHz), 107 (C-Band). Claims small business bidding credits. Part of small cadre of individual spectrum speculators alongside Rajendra and Neera Singh (High Band License). LLC name is likely his street address.

name: Qualcomm Incorporated
industry_description: Wireless technology company. Holds some spectrum licenses. Major bid in 700 MHz auction. Developer of key wireless patents and chipsets.

name: Clearwire Corporation
industry_description: Held massive 2.5 GHz spectrum portfolio. Backed by Sprint, Intel, Comcast, Google, TWC. WiMAX then LTE. Acquired by Sprint 2013.

name: Sprint Corporation
industry_description: Former fourth-largest US carrier. Held massive 2.5 GHz spectrum portfolio via Clearwire. Merged with T-Mobile in 2020.

name: DISH Network Corporation
industry_description: Massive spectrum hoarder turned carrier. Spent over $25B on spectrum across multiple auctions through shell companies. Building Open RAN 5G network under DoJ mandate.

name: Comcast Corporation
industry_description: Largest US cable company. Entered wireless via Xfinity Mobile (MVNO on Verizon). Acquired spectrum in 600 MHz and CBRS auctions.

name: Charter Communications
industry_description: Second largest US cable company (Spectrum brand). Entered wireless via Spectrum Mobile MVNO. CBRS spectrum holder.

name: Anterix Inc.
industry_description: RESOLVED: NASDAQ: ATEX. Woodland Park NJ. Lineage: FleetCall (1987) → Nextel (1993, spun off separately) → Pacific DataVision → pdvWireless (2016) → Anterix (Aug 2019). Founded by Morgan O'Brien and Brian McAuley — BOTH co-founders of Nextel Communications. O'Brien co-founded the company that CREATED the 900 MHz market (Nextel), then acquired that same spectrum back years later. Largest licensed 900 MHz holder nationwide (896-901/935-940 MHz). Acquired Sprint's 900 MHz licenses 2014. Business: 30-year spectrum leases to electric utilities for private LTE. Customers: Ameren, Evergy, LCRA, SDG&E, Tampa Electric, Xcel Energy, Southern Company, NYPA, Hawaiian Electric, Duke Energy, Exelon. FCC approved 900 MHz LMR→LTE transition May 2020. Jan 2025: FCC NPRM to expand to 5+5 MHz. DIRECT OPPONENT of NextNav's 902-928 MHz petition.

name: AT&T Inc.
industry_description: Second largest US wireless carrier. Major spectrum holder across all bands. Largest AWS-3 bidder at $18.2B. FirstNet nationwide public safety network operator.

name: Columbia Capital
industry_description: RESOLVED: 204 South Union St, Alexandria VA. Founded 1989. Managing Partners: Jim Fleming (since 1994), John Siegel (since 2000). $9.0B total fund commitments. 175+ portfolio investments. MOST PROLIFIC non-carrier spectrum PE investor. Auction vehicles: Three Forty-Five Spectrum (Auction 110, $1.4B), High Band Holdings (Auction 103, mmWave), AB Spectrum (sold to T-Mobile), LB Spectrum Holdings (acquired from AT&T), Channel 51 (19.79% equity). Among FIRST INVESTORS in Nextel (1987). Early investor in XM Satellite Radio. Board seats at Omnispace (satellite spectrum). Fleming and Siegel control all vehicles through Columbia Capital entity structure.

name: AT&T Inc.
industry_description: Largest US telecom conglomerate. Provides wireless, broadband, and enterprise services. Operates FirstNet public safety network under 25-year federal contract. Historically the most cooperative telecom partner with NSA surveillance programs including FAIRVIEW, Room 641A, and PRISM. Acquired Straight Path, Cricket/Leap Wireless, DirecTV. In 2025 acquired $23B in EchoStar/Dish spectrum.

name: AT&T Mobility LLC
industry_description: Wireless operating subsidiary of AT&T. Formerly Cingular Wireless LLC. Holds the majority of AT&T wireless spectrum licenses in FCC ULS. Largest holder of licensed spectrum in the US by MHz-POPs after 2025 EchoStar acquisition.

name: Cricket Wireless LLC
industry_description: Prepaid wireless brand owned by AT&T. Originally an independent carrier (Leap Wireless International), acquired by AT&T in March 2014 for $1.19B. Merged with AT&T Aio Wireless operations.

name: FirstNet Authority
industry_description: Independent federal authority within NTIA/Department of Commerce. Oversees the nationwide public safety broadband network (Band 14, 758-768/788-798 MHz). Awarded 25-year contract to AT&T in March 2017. $8B 10-year expansion investment announced 2024. Serves first responders at federal, state, and local levels.

name: Verizon Communications Inc.
industry_description: Second-largest US wireless carrier. Operates Verizon Wireless (Cellco Partnership), Verizon Business, Verizon Consumer. Major mmWave spectrum holder via Straight Path ($3.1B) and XO Communications acquisitions. Acquired TracFone ($6.25B, 2021). Provides critical DoD and federal government network services.

name: Cellco Partnership
industry_description: Legal operating entity for Verizon Wireless. Holds the vast majority of Verizon wireless spectrum licenses in FCC ULS. Partnership between Verizon subsidiaries after 2014 Vodafone buyout.

name: Straight Path Communications Inc.
industry_description: Acquired by Verizon 2018 for $3.1B. Held critical 28 GHz and 39 GHz mmWave licenses. Formerly IDT Corporation subsidiary.

name: TracFone Wireless Inc.
industry_description: Largest US prepaid wireless provider. Acquired by Verizon from America Movil for $6.25B in November 2021. Operates Straight Talk, Total Wireless, TracFone, SafeLink, and other prepaid brands. ~20M subscribers at acquisition.

name: FiberTower Corporation
industry_description: Acquired by AT&T for 24/39 GHz mmWave spectrum. Bankrupt 2012, emerged 2014. Crown Castle early investor.

name: T-Mobile US Inc.
industry_description: Third-largest US wireless carrier. Subsidiary of Deutsche Telekom AG (majority owner). Completed Sprint merger April 2020 for $26B, gaining massive 2.5 GHz mid-band spectrum holdings. Acquired MetroPCS via reverse takeover in 2013. Acquired Layer3 TV (now T-Mobile Home Internet). Acquired UScellular wireless operations August 2025 for $4.4B.

name: Metro by T-Mobile
industry_description: Prepaid wireless brand owned by T-Mobile. Formerly MetroPCS, an independent carrier. T-Mobile acquired MetroPCS via reverse takeover in 2013, which gave T-Mobile its NASDAQ listing. Rebranded to Metro by T-Mobile in 2018.

name: Sprint Corporation
industry_description: Former fourth-largest US wireless carrier. Merged into T-Mobile April 2020 for $26B. Held massive 2.5 GHz spectrum portfolio (via Clearwire acquisition) plus 800 MHz and PCS spectrum. Sprint brand discontinued. Key spectrum holdings now form backbone of T-Mobile 5G mid-band strategy. Previously owned by SoftBank Group.

name: Dish Network Corporation
industry_description: Satellite TV and wireless company. Merged with EchoStar Dec 31, 2023. Accumulated massive spectrum portfolio over 15+ years through auctions and shell company bidding strategies. Used shell companies extensively to claim designated entity bidding credits. FCC found Dish controlled Northstar and SNR, denying $3.3B in bidding credits. Sold $23B in spectrum to AT&T (2025) and spectrum to SpaceX. Shut down wireless network Nov 2025, became AT&T MVNO.

name: EchoStar Corporation
industry_description: Parent company of Dish Network after Dec 31, 2023 merger. Satellite communications, wireless, and streaming company. Controls Hughes Network Systems (satellite broadband). After merger, held combined spectrum portfolio worth $40B+ before divestitures. Sold majority of spectrum to AT&T ($23B) and SpaceX. Shut down Boost Mobile 5G network Nov 2025.

name: Northstar Wireless LLC
industry_description: RESOLVED — DISH DESIGNATED ENTITY SCHEME: Northstar Spectrum LLC owned 85% by DISH (via American AWS-3 Wireless II LLC), 15% by Northstar Manager LLC (connected to Doyon Ltd, Alaska Native Corp). Gross bids $7.85B, claimed 25% DE credit = $5.88B net. FCC DENIED $3.3B in combined credits (with SNR), finding DISH exercised de facto control through ~24 separate contracts. D.C. Circuit upheld. Licenses largely surrendered. Most notorious DE abuse case in FCC history.

name: SNR Wireless LicenseCo LLC
industry_description: RESOLVED — DISH DESIGNATED ENTITY SCHEME: 85% owned by DISH (via American AWS-3 Wireless III LLC). Controlled by John Muleta — FORMER CHIEF OF FCC WIRELESS TELECOMMUNICATIONS BUREAU (2003-2005). Revolving door: the man running DISH's DE vehicle was the former head of the FCC bureau that oversees spectrum auctions. Gross bids $5.48B. FCC denied credits. Licenses surrendered.

name: ParkerB.com Wireless LLC
industry_description: Dish Network-controlled entity used for spectrum license holdings. Named after Parker, Colorado street near Dish HQ. Part of Dish constellation of spectrum holding entities.

name: Weminuche LLC
industry_description: Dish Network-controlled spectrum holding entity. Named after Weminuche Wilderness in Colorado. Part of pattern of Colorado-themed shell companies used by Dish for spectrum accumulation.

name: Wetterhorn Wireless LLC
industry_description: Dish Network-controlled spectrum holding entity. Named after Wetterhorn Peak in Colorado. Part of Dish pattern of using Colorado geographic names for shell companies.

name: Crestone Peak Wireless LLC
industry_description: Dish Network-controlled spectrum holding entity. Named after Crestone Peak in Colorado Sangre de Cristo range. Used for spectrum license filings with FCC.

name: Maroon Bells Wireless LLC
industry_description: Dish Network-controlled spectrum holding entity. Named after Maroon Bells peaks near Aspen, Colorado. Part of extensive Dish shell company network for spectrum holdings.

name: Comcast Corporation
industry_description: Largest US cable company and ISP. Operates Xfinity (cable/broadband) and Xfinity Mobile (Verizon MVNO). Holds CBRS spectrum licenses. Former SpectrumCo LLC partner (sold AWS-1 spectrum to Verizon for $3.6B in 2012). Sold $3.3B in 600 MHz spectrum to T-Mobile in 2023. Also owns NBCUniversal.

name: SpectrumCo LLC
industry_description: Joint venture between Comcast, Time Warner Cable, and Bright House Networks formed to bid on AWS-1 spectrum in FCC Auction 66 (2006). Won 137 licenses for $2.37B. Sold all AWS-1 spectrum to Verizon for $3.6B in 2012 as part of deal that included MVNO agreements allowing cable companies to resell Verizon wireless. JV dissolved after spectrum sale.

name: Charter Communications Inc.
industry_description: Second-largest US cable company. Operates under Spectrum brand. Spectrum Mobile is Verizon MVNO launched 2018. Acquired Time Warner Cable and Bright House Networks in 2016 for $78.7B. Services in 41 states, 57M homes. Announced merger with Cox Communications in 2025. Limited owned spectrum — primarily operates as MVNO.

name: Cox Communications Inc.
industry_description: Third-largest US cable company. Privately held (Cox Enterprises). Operates Cox Mobile (Verizon MVNO). Spent $212M on CBRS spectrum in 2020. Operates Cox Private Networks for enterprise/utility customers using CBRS and other spectrum. Announced merger with Charter Communications in 2025.

name: US Cellular Corporation
industry_description: Regional wireless carrier serving rural and suburban markets. Subsidiary of Telephone and Data Systems (TDS). Held significant spectrum across 600 MHz, 700 MHz, AWS, PCS, 2.5 GHz, and 24 GHz bands. In 2024-2025, sold wireless operations and ~30% of spectrum to T-Mobile for $4.4B. Sold additional spectrum to AT&T ($1.018B) and Verizon (~$1B). Retaining ~30% of spectrum and all 4,400 towers.

name: Anterix Inc.
industry_description: Largest holder of licensed 900 MHz spectrum (896-901/935-940 MHz) across contiguous US, Alaska, Hawaii, and Puerto Rico. Focuses exclusively on leasing spectrum to electric utilities for private LTE/5G broadband networks supporting grid modernization. FCC expanded 900 MHz broadband allocation from 6 MHz to 10 MHz. Customers include Ameren, Evergy, Xcel Energy, Oncor ($102M deal), LCRA.

name: Rivada Networks Inc.
industry_description: RESOLVED: Founded July 2004. CEO: Declan Ganley (Irish businessman). PRIMARY FUNDER: Peter Thiel (PayPal). LOBBYIST+INVESTOR: Karl Rove. BOARD: Gen Richard Myers (15th Chairman Joint Chiefs of Staff), Martin O'Malley (fmr MD Governor), a former LTG, two former admirals, a former Deputy Secretary of Homeland Security. Failed DOD 5G no-bid spectrum lease attempt (2019-2020) — Karl Rove lobbied Senate Armed Services Committee, WH Chief of Staff Meadows pushed, Newt Gingrich promoted. AT&T/Verizon called it "nationalization of 5G." CREW sued DOD for records. Plan killed. Rivada Mercury (2016): partnered Harris/Ericsson/Nokia for FirstNet bid, lost. Rivada Space Networks (2022): $2.4B contract with Terran Orbital for 300 LEO satellites, must launch 288 by Sept 2026 for ITU Ka-band rights. Execution risk HIGH.

name: Rivada Port Graham Solutions LLC
industry_description: Joint venture between Rivada Networks and Port Graham Development Corporation (Alaska Native Corporation). SBA-certified small business. Second-most successful vendor under original TACCOM contract and most successful small business awardee. Provides tactical communications maintenance and support for DHS.

name: Port Graham Corporation
industry_description: Alaska Native Corporation (ANC) formed under Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act of 1971. Represents the Sugpiag people of the Kenai Peninsula. Parent of family of SBA 8(a) certified subsidiary companies. JV partner with Rivada Networks in Rivada Port Graham Solutions.

name: Rivada Space Networks GmbH
industry_description: Wholly owned subsidiary of Rivada Networks planning 600 LEO satellite constellation with laser crosslinks for global mesh networking. CEO: Declan Ganley. COO: Mark Rigolle. $2.4B contract with Terran Orbital/Tyvak for 300 satellites (288 + 12 spares, 500kg each). HQ in Munich, Germany.

name: Ligado Networks LLC
industry_description: RESOLVED: Reston VA. Corporate lineage: American Mobile Satellite Corp (1988) → Motient → Mobile Satellite Ventures (2001) → SkyTerra (2008) → LightSquared (2010, Harbinger/Falcone) → Ligado (Dec 2015, post-bankruptcy). Post-2015 owners: Fortress Investment Group, Centerbridge Partners, JPMorgan Chase (controlling); Harbinger/Falcone (minority). Board chair: Ivan Seidenberg (FORMER VERIZON CEO — ironic given Verizon opposed LightSquared). ~40 MHz L-band spectrum adjacent to GPS. GPS INTERFERENCE SAGA: Jan 2011 studies showed severe jamming. Opposition from DOD, DOT, DHS, FAA, Lockheed Martin, Garmin, Trimble, Bradford Parkinson (GPS architect). FCC approved modified app April 2020 despite DOD/NTIA opposition. NEVER deployed, NEVER generated revenue. SECOND BANKRUPTCY filed Jan 5, 2025 — $8.6B debt, ZERO revenue. SUING US GOVERNMENT alleging DOD illegally uses Ligado spectrum for "secret DoD systems" — seeking $39 BILLION. AST SpaceMobile deal: spectrum usage rights for 45 MHz L-band, 80+ years, for $550M (direct-to-device satellite). $8.6B in debt converted to equity. Most dramatic failure in US spectrum history.

name: L3Harris Technologies Inc.
industry_description: Sixth-largest US defense contractor. Formed from 2019 merger of L3 Technologies and Harris Corporation. $18B annual revenue, 48,000 employees, customers in 100+ countries. Core capabilities: tactical radios, electronic warfare, ISR, spectrum dominance, satellite communications, night vision, C3ISR systems. Does not hold commercial spectrum but is primary provider of military spectrum management and electronic warfare systems.

name: Raytheon Technologies Corporation
industry_description: World largest aerospace and defense company (rebranded from Raytheon Technologies to RTX). Produces radars, missiles, satellites, communications systems, navigation systems, jammers. Critical interest in radar spectrum allocations — advocates for sharing not vacating federal spectrum. Uses UHF (420-430 MHz), L-band (1435-1525 MHz), C-band (5400-5900 MHz), S-band (2200-2390 MHz) for missile testing and military systems. Holds FCC experimental licenses for weapons testing.

name: Lockheed Martin Corporation
industry_description: Largest US defense contractor by revenue (~$68B). Builds GPS III/IIIF satellites under $7.2B+ contract with US Space Force. GPS constellation is the foundation of military and civilian position/navigation/timing (PNT). Also builds military satellite communications systems, F-35, missile defense. Spectrum interests center on protecting GPS frequencies and military satellite bands.

name: Science Applications International Corporation
industry_description: Government technology services company. 98% of revenue from government customers, 52% from DoD. Provides IT modernization, signals intelligence systems, electronic warfare, cybersecurity, and border security technology. Acquired Scitor Holdings ($790M, 2015) to expand intelligence community presence through classified contracts.

name: Leidos Holdings Inc.
industry_description: Defense, intelligence, and health IT company. Split from original SAIC in 2013. Acquired Lockheed Martin IT division ($4.6B, 2016). Major intelligence community contractor. Provides cyber, analytics, software, and systems integration for defense and intelligence customers.

name: General Dynamics Corporation
industry_description: Fifth-largest US defense contractor. General Dynamics Mission Systems (GDMS) provides secure communications, tactical radios, satellite technology, and cybersecurity for defense and intelligence. Prime contractor for Army Warfighter Information Network-Tactical (WIN-T). Sold GDSatcom Technologies to CPI ($175M, 2020). Also builds submarines (Electric Boat), tanks (Land Systems), and Gulfstream jets.

name: Grain Management LLC
industry_description: Leading spectrum-focused investment firm founded 2007. Specializes in digital infrastructure: fiber, wireless spectrum, data centers, cell towers. Manages $3.75B+ across multiple funds. Portfolio includes NewLevel (600 MHz), NewLevel II (C-band), NewLevel III (3.45 GHz). Acquired 800 MHz spectrum from T-Mobile for utility use with Black & Veatch. Spectrum Holdings III fund: $1.5B. GCOF III fund: $2.25B.

name: NewLevel LLC
industry_description: Grain Management portfolio company holding 600 MHz spectrum licenses. Acquired 26 licenses in FCC Auction 1000 (600 MHz incentive auction) covering fastest-growing markets in top 50 US metro areas including Orlando, Portland, and Tampa. Licenses leased to T-Mobile as part of spectrum swap arrangement.

name: NewLevel II LP
industry_description: Grain Management portfolio company holding C-band (3.7-3.98 GHz) spectrum licenses from FCC Auction 107. Fifth-highest bidder overall at $1.277B for 10 licenses covering well-populated US markets. Prime mid-band 5G spectrum.

name: Columbia Capital LLC
industry_description: Private equity firm specializing in communications, media, and digital infrastructure for 30+ years. Major spectrum investor: backed AB Spectrum (700 MHz A Block, sold to T-Mobile 2016), Channel 51 ($859M in 600 MHz auction), and Three Forty-Five Spectrum LLC ($1.4B in 3.45 GHz auction). Also invests in data centers, towers, small cells, fiber. Team includes Monish Kundra (spectrum specialist).

name: Three Forty-Five Spectrum LLC
industry_description: Columbia Capital-backed entity formed to acquire 3.45 GHz spectrum in FCC Auction 110 (2022). Biggest winner outside Big 4 carriers, spending $1.4B for 18 licenses across 11 largest markets (weighted average 5 MHz). Only non-carrier bidder to exceed $1B in the auction. Holds prime mid-band 5G spectrum in top markets.

name: Starry Group Holdings Inc.
industry_description: Fixed wireless ISP using 37 GHz and 24 GHz mmWave spectrum. Founded 2014, went public 2022. Filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy February 2023, emerged May 2023 as private company. Now focused on 5 core markets: Boston, NYC, Washington DC, Denver, Los Angeles. Uses 37 GHz in primary markets, 24 GHz (won in FCC 2019 auction) in Columbus. Subsequently acquired by Verizon.

name: NextNav Inc.
industry_description: RESOLVED: McLean VA. NASDAQ: NN. Went public Oct 2021 via Spartacus SPAC ($1.2B EV, $408M gross). CEO: Mariam Sorond. BOARD: Gary Parsons (fmr Sirius XM chair), Bandel Carano (Oak Investment Partners), RADM H. Wyman Howard USN(Ret) (appointed May 2025), RADM Lorin Selby USN(Ret) (appointed May 2025), Lisa Hook (Technology and National Defense Committee). INVESTORS: Goldman Sachs Asset Management, Fortress Investment Group, Oak Investment Partners, Koch Strategic Platforms, Ophir Asset Management. Technology: Metropolitan Beacon System/TerraPoiNT — terrestrial 3D PNT (GPS backup). Acquired 4 MHz in 902-928 MHz from Telesaurus Holdings for up to $50M. Filed FCC Docket 24-240 to reconfigure lower 900 MHz for 15 MHz 5G broadband + PNT allocation. Two admirals added to board 2025 + "National Defense" committee = deliberate strategy to frame 902 MHz petition as national security.

name: Doyon Limited
industry_description: Alaska Native Regional Corporation representing Athabascan people of interior Alaska. Largest private landowner in Alaska (12.5M acres). Partnered with Dish Network via Northstar Wireless LLC to claim designated entity status in FCC AWS-3 Auction 97. FCC found Dish maintained de facto control of the partnership, making Northstar ineligible for $3.3B in bidding credits.

name: Dish Wireless LLC
industry_description: Wireless operating subsidiary of EchoStar/Dish. Held spectrum licenses across AWS-3, AWS-4, H Block, 700 MHz, and 600 MHz bands. Committed to 5G network buildout as condition of Sprint/T-Mobile merger approval. Requested buildout extensions to Dec 2026/June 2028. Network shut down November 2025, became AT&T MVNO.

name: Telephone and Data Systems Inc.
industry_description: Parent company of US Cellular Corporation and TDS Telecom. Family-controlled (LeRoy T. Carlson family). Announced sale of UScellular wireless operations and spectrum to T-Mobile, AT&T, and Verizon totaling $6.4B+ in 2024-2025. Retaining UScellular towers (4,400) and TDS Telecom wireline/fiber business.

name: Deutsche Telekom AG
industry_description: German telecommunications giant and majority owner of T-Mobile US. Federal Republic of Germany holds ~30% stake via KfW. Largest European telecom by revenue. T-Mobile US is primary growth engine. Foreign ownership creates CFIUS/national security review requirements for US defense-related spectrum matters.

name: Harbinger Capital Partners
industry_description: Hedge fund founded by Philip Falcone. Invested ~$2.9B into LightSquared/Ligado Networks L-band spectrum venture. After LightSquared bankruptcy, Falcone was barred from securities industry by SEC for self-dealing (using fund assets for personal tax obligations). Harbinger retained minority ownership in reorganized Ligado Networks. One of the most expensive spectrum speculation failures in history.

name: Centerbridge Partners LP
industry_description: Private investment firm. Major post-bankruptcy owner of Ligado Networks after 2015 restructuring. Controls Ligado alongside Fortress Investment Group and JPMorgan Chase.

name: Fortress Investment Group LLC
industry_description: Investment management firm. Subsidiary of SoftBank Group. Major post-bankruptcy owner of Ligado Networks after 2015 restructuring alongside Centerbridge Partners and JPMorgan Chase.

name: AB Spectrum LLC
industry_description: Columbia Capital portfolio company formed to acquire and operate 700 MHz A Block spectrum licenses. Wireless assets sold to T-Mobile in 2016.

name: Channel 51 License Co LLC
industry_description: Columbia Capital-financed bidder in FCC 600 MHz Incentive Auction (Auction 1001). Spent $859M to acquire 8 licenses. Named after UHF TV Channel 51 (the band being repurposed in the incentive auction).

name: Hughes Network Systems LLC
industry_description: Satellite broadband provider. Subsidiary of EchoStar. Operates Jupiter satellite fleet for HughesNet consumer and enterprise broadband. Provides satellite services to DoD and federal agencies. Key asset in EchoStar portfolio alongside Dish spectrum holdings.

name: Clearwire Corporation
industry_description: Former holder of massive 2.5 GHz spectrum portfolio. Originally a Craig McCaw/Intel/Sprint joint venture. Sprint acquired full control 2013. Spectrum passed to T-Mobile via Sprint merger 2020. The 2.5 GHz holdings are now the backbone of T-Mobile mid-band 5G strategy nationwide.

name: SoftBank Group Corp.
industry_description: Japanese multinational conglomerate. Former majority owner of Sprint Corporation (acquired 2013, $21.6B). Sprint merged into T-Mobile 2020, giving SoftBank significant T-Mobile stake. Also owns Fortress Investment Group (Ligado Networks investor), ARM Holdings, and Vision Fund portfolio. Major indirect spectrum stakeholder through T-Mobile ownership.

name: SpaceX
industry_description: Elon Musk-founded aerospace and satellite company. Operates Starlink LEO satellite constellation. Acquired spectrum from EchoStar as part of $42.6B divestiture. Starlink provides satellite broadband globally and is pursuing direct-to-cell capabilities. Major DoD launch provider. Considering becoming mobile carrier.

name: Palantir Technologies Inc.
industry_description: Data analytics platform company co-founded by Peter Thiel. Provides Gotham (intelligence) and Foundry (commercial) platforms. Major IC and DoD contractor. Connected to spectrum world through Peter Thiel investment in Rivada Networks. Does not hold spectrum but processes signals intelligence and communications data.

name: Cox Enterprises Inc.
industry_description: Privately held conglomerate. Parent of Cox Communications (cable/ISP) and Cox Automotive (Autotrader, Kelley Blue Book, Manheim). Family-controlled (Cox family). One of largest private companies in US.

name: Xfinity Mobile
industry_description: Comcast mobile MVNO brand launched 2017. Operates on Verizon network under MVNO agreement originating from SpectrumCo LLC spectrum sale. Supplements with Comcast-owned CBRS spectrum and Wi-Fi for data offload. Growing rapidly as cable companies leverage bundling with broadband.

name: Spectrum Mobile
industry_description: Charter Communications mobile MVNO brand launched June 2018. Operates on Verizon network. Available only to Spectrum Internet customers. Leverages Charter cable Wi-Fi network for data offload.

name: Boost Mobile
industry_description: Prepaid wireless brand. Originally Sprint subsidiary. Divested to Dish Network as condition of T-Mobile/Sprint merger for $1.4B (2020). Became EchoStar subsidiary after Dish merger (2023). Attempted 5G network operation, shut down Nov 2025. Now operates as AT&T MVNO.

name: Visible Service LLC
industry_description: All-digital prepaid wireless brand on Verizon network. Launched 2018. Positioned as digital-first, app-managed MVNO. Rebranded as Visible by Verizon in 2022.

name: America Movil SAB de CV
industry_description: Mexican telecommunications giant controlled by Carlos Slim. Former owner of TracFone Wireless. Sold TracFone to Verizon for $6.25B in 2021. Largest wireless carrier in Latin America. Operates Claro, Telcel brands.

name: Northrop Grumman Corporation
industry_description: Fourth-largest US defense contractor. Builds stealth bombers (B-21), missile defense systems (Ground-based Midcourse Defense), and space systems. Acquired Orbital ATK (now Northrop Grumman Space Systems). Spectrum interests in military satellite communications, radar, and electronic warfare. Acquired Spectrum Astro (Lockheed GPS III partner).

name: In-Q-Tel Inc.
industry_description: CIA-backed strategic venture capital firm. Invests in technology companies that serve intelligence community needs. Seed investor in Palantir Technologies (Peter Thiel co-founded). Invests in companies across cybersecurity, data analytics, autonomous systems, and communications. Bridge between IC requirements and commercial technology.

name: Iridium Communications Inc.
industry_description: Operates 66-satellite LEO constellation providing global voice and data communications. L-band spectrum neighbor to Ligado Networks. Actively opposed Ligado terrestrial operations due to interference concerns. Key Dept of Defense communications provider via EMSS (Enhanced Mobile Satellite Services) contract.

name: Qualcomm Incorporated
industry_description: Semiconductor and wireless technology company. Invented CDMA. Designs Snapdragon mobile chips used in most smartphones. Holds fundamental patents on wireless standards (3G, 4G, 5G). Participated in FCC spectrum auctions. Major influence on spectrum policy through standards development and licensing. Defense electronics via subsidiary.

name: Boingo Wireless Inc.
industry_description: RESOLVED: Frisco TX (relocated from LA 2023). Founded 2001 by Sky Dayton (EarthLink co-founder). Acquired by DigitalBridge (fka Colony Capital) June 2021 for $854M (delisted from NASDAQ: WIFI). CEO: Mike Finley (former Qualcomm exec, also DigitalBridge Senior Advisor). MILITARY CONTRACTS: Long-term with AAFES (Army/Air Force Exchange) and MCCS (Marine Corps Community Services). WiFi/DAS/IPTV/broadband across 100+ US military installations worldwide including Japan and South Korea. 2,000+ buildings at 60+ Army, Air Force, Marine Corps, FBI, DHS Training Center locations. GSA Schedule contract for 5G/DAS/WiFi for federal government. CBRS deployments at airports and venues. Dominant military base connectivity provider — inherent proximity to sensitive defense operations.

name: DigitalBridge Group Inc.
industry_description: RESOLVED: Digital infrastructure PE firm. Formerly Colony Capital (Tom Barrack, resigned). CEO: Marc Ganzi. Acquired Boingo Wireless 2021 for $854M. Boingo provides connectivity on 100+ US military bases. Major tower/fiber/data center investor globally.

name: Globalstar Inc.
industry_description: LEO satellite communications company. Holds spectrum in S-band and L-band. Apple invested $1.5B for satellite SOS capabilities in iPhones. Also holds TLPS (Terrestrial Low Power Service) authority for n53 band (2483.5-2495 MHz), used for satellite-to-phone and IoT applications.

name: Viasat Inc.
industry_description: Satellite broadband and defense communications company. Acquired Inmarsat ($7.3B, 2023) gaining L-band and Ka-band global satellite spectrum. Major defense contractor providing tactical data links (Link 16), satellite terminals, and cybersecurity. Now operates combined GEO/LEO/HEO constellation.

name: Lumen Technologies Inc.
industry_description: Formerly CenturyLink. Major fiber backbone operator with one of largest long-haul fiber networks globally. Provides enterprise and government network services. Sold off consumer/CLEC operations to focus on enterprise fiber and edge computing. Critical internet backbone provider.

name: American Tower Corporation
industry_description: Second-largest US cell tower company and largest global tower company. Owns ~43,000 US sites and ~220,000 international sites. REIT structure. Spun off from American Radio Systems in 1998. Critical infrastructure for all wireless carriers. Dish Network/EchoStar dispute with American Tower over tower lease obligations during spectrum selloff.

name: Alaska Native Wireless (Auction 35)
industry_description: CIRI-affiliated designated entity using Alaska Native bidding credits for spectrum auctions.

name: Pocket Communications Inc. (Auction 58)
industry_description: Won PCS licenses in Auction 58 after 7 years trying. Built San Antonio network.

name: Bell Atlantic Mobile
industry_description: Bell Atlantic's wireless subsidiary. Bidder in Auctions 4, 11. Became Verizon Wireless.

name: NYNEX Mobile Communications
industry_description: NYNEX's wireless arm. Auction 4 bidder via PCS PrimeCo JV.

name: US West NewVector Group
industry_description: US West's wireless subsidiary. Part of PCS PrimeCo consortium.

name: SBC Communications
industry_description: Parent of Southwestern Bell, Pacific Bell, Ameritech wireless. Bidder across auctions.

name: GTE Corporation
industry_description: Largest independent telco. Wireless operations bid in PCS auctions.

name: Craig O. McCaw
industry_description: Pioneer spectrum investor. Behind McCaw Cellular, NextLink/XO, Clearwire, Eagle River Investments.

name: John Stanton
industry_description: Co-founder Western Wireless and VoiceStream. Built T-Mobile's US spectrum base.

name: Allen Salmasi
industry_description: Founder of NextWave Telecom. Qualcomm CDMA pioneer turned spectrum bidder.

name: Paul Allen
industry_description: Microsoft co-founder. Invested in spectrum through Vulcan, Charter, and other vehicles.

name: General Electric Capital Corporation
industry_description: GE's finance arm. Applied for PCS auctions but did not win major licenses.

name: TeleCommunications Inc. (TCI)
industry_description: Largest US cable company. Sprint PCS consortium partner.

name: Cox Communications
industry_description: Cable company. Sprint PCS consortium partner for wireless.

name: Blaze Broadband, LLC
industry_description: DEAD END: Small broadband provider. No parent company identified.

name: Plateau Telecommunications Inc.
industry_description: Rural New Mexico cellular carrier. Won unserved cellular licenses.

name: Salmon River Communications
industry_description: Rural Idaho telco. Associated with Salmon PCS.

name: Sunesys Inc.
industry_description: Fiber/spectrum company. Bid in multiple early auctions.

name: Central Texas Telephone Cooperative
industry_description: Rural telco. Won D/E/F block PCS licenses.

name: Northeast Oklahoma Telephone Co.
industry_description: Rural telco. Auction 11 bidder.

name: Oklahoma Western Telephone Co.
industry_description: Rural telco. Auction 11 bidder.

name: Choice Phone LLC
industry_description: Designated entity PCS winner. Actually attempted operations.

name: Ntelos Inc.
industry_description: Virginia-based wireless carrier. Won D/E/F PCS licenses.

name: Bluegrass Cellular
industry_description: Kentucky-based regional wireless. PCS license holder.

name: Commnet Cellular Inc.
industry_description: Rural wireless operator. Won PCS licenses in western US.

name: Highland Cellular Inc.
industry_description: Rural Virginia wireless carrier. PCS bidder.

name: SureWest Communications
industry_description: California CLEC/wireless. PCS bidder.

name: Moise Advisory LLC
industry_description: Private investment advisory — Edward Moise Jr. Created Cherry Wireless (Auction 110) and Canopy Spectrum (Auction 107) as auction bidding vehicles.

name: Aeolus Spectrum Management LLC
industry_description: Charles C. "Charlie" Townsend — serial spectrum investor. Career: Providence Journal Telecom CEO (1986), founded Atlantic Cellular (1989, sold to Rural Cellular 1998), founded Hawaiian Wireless (1996), President/CEO Aloha Partners LP (became largest UHF-TV owner in US, sold 700 MHz to AT&T 2008). Created Whitewater Wireless as next vehicle. Board member of Gogo Inc (in-flight connectivity). Pattern: acquire at auction, sell to AT&T.

name: C Spire Wireless
industry_description: Regional wireless carrier — Mississippi/Southeast. Formerly Cellular South. Rebranded to C Spire 2011.

name: Shenandoah Telecommunications
industry_description: Shentel — Virginia regional telecom. Acquired NTELOS/King Street Wireless 2016. Had Sprint MVNO agreement, terminated when T-Mobile merged with Sprint. Now independent carrier.

name: High Band Holdings LLC
industry_description: Columbia Capital auction vehicle for mmWave Auction 103 (37/39/47 GHz). Part of Columbia Capital's multi-decade spectrum accumulation strategy.
